title: Make SaveHeartbeats transactional + batched (pgx.Batch/CopyFrom)
description: Audit finding (gaka-u45): SaveHeartbeats (internal/db, formerly sessions.go:227-296) is not transactional and is N+1 on the hottest write path — one INSERT INTO projects per unique pair + one QueryRow per heartbeat + per-sender RecomputeGaps/RefreshRollup. title: Detect + warn on wakatime.com API schema drift during import
description: We depend on the wakatime.com API shape for /import (heartbeats, user_agents, machine_names). If Wakatime changes their domain model/response schema, imports could silently drop or mangle fields. Add drift detection: during an import run, validate fetched payloads against the expected schema (e.g. strict-decode with unknown-field detection, required-field checks) and surface a WARNING banner on the Import page when drift is detected (unknown fields, missing expected fields, type changes), with details of what drifted. Import should still proceed where safe (warn, don't hard-fail, unless required fields are gone). Optionally persist drift findings on the import_jobs record so past runs show it.
design:
# Plan: Detect + warn on wakatime.com API schema drift during import (gaka-unq.1)

## Context (as-built)

- Fetch/decode: `internal/importer/importer.go` — `getJSON()` (json.Decoder straight into typed structs), called from `fetchLookups()` (user_agents, machine_names), `importDay()` (heartbeats), and `FetchAllTimeRange()`. Response structs: `importHeartbeat`/`heartbeatList`, `userAgent(List)`, `machineName(List)`, `allTimeResponse`.
- Job model: `internal/db/importjobs.go` (`db.Job`, `jobColumns`, `scanJob`); table from migrations `00005`/`00006` (`import_jobs`, `import_job_logs`). Job JSON is the shared FE contract and flows through REST (`internal/handler/import.go`: ImportJobs/ImportJob) AND the WS stream (Hub `Event{Type, Job, Log}` in `internal/importer/hub.go`, snapshot + progress/state events in `ImportJobWS`).
- FE: `web/src/pages/Import.tsx` + `web/src/components/import/{CurrentRunPanel,RunDetail,HistoryList,LogTerminal,ImportStateBadge}.tsx`, socket in `web/src/hooks/useImportJobSocket.ts`, types in `web/src/types/api.ts` (`ImportJob`).

## Approach: decode-into-map diff with per-endpoint schema specs (NOT DisallowUnknownFields)

Rationale:
- `json.Decoder.DisallowUnknownFields` aborts on the FIRST unknown field and turns decode into a hard error — the opposite of "warn, don't fail". It also gives no structured findings and cannot express a benign-field allowlist.
- Raw-message byte comparison against a golden payload is too brittle (ordering, values change every response).
- Chosen: for each response, unmarshal the envelope as `struct{ Data []json.RawMessage }` (plus keep the existing typed decode), then diff a SAMPLE of items against a `schemaSpec` per endpoint:
  - `known` field set: json tags of the typed struct (built once via reflection or hand-listed).
  - `baseline` allowlist: fields wakatime returns today that we deliberately don't map (heartbeats: `id`, `created_at`, `machine_name_id` is mapped, but e.g. `project_root_count`, etc. — capture a live response to seed this; see Open Questions). CRITICAL: without this, unknown-field detection fires on 100% of imports.
  - `required` set: heartbeats `entity`, `type`, `time`, `user_agent_id`; user_agents/machine_names `id`, `value`; envelope requires `data`.
  - Type check: for each present known field, `json.Unmarshal(fragment, new(FieldType))`; failure => `type_changed`.
- Sampling: check the first N=5 items of each day's heartbeat payload (schema is uniform per response); lookups are small — check all. Findings are deduped by `(endpoint, kind, field)` with a running count, so repeated drift across 1000 days produces one finding with count.

## Backend changes

1. New file `internal/importer/drift.go`
   - `type DriftFinding struct { Endpoint, Kind, Field, Detail, Severity string; FirstSeenDay string; Count int }` with JSON tags (camelCase to match FE contract). Kinds: `unknown_field`, `missing_required`, `type_changed`, `envelope_changed`. Severity: `warning` | `error` (error == required field gone / envelope broken).
   - `type driftCollector` (per-job, single goroutine — no locking): `check(endpoint, day string, raw []byte, spec schemaSpec)`, `findings() []DriftFinding`, `hasNew() bool` (to emit a log line only on first occurrence).
   - `schemaSpec` values for `heartbeats`, `user_agents`, `machine_names` (+ optionally `all_time_since_today`).
2. `internal/importer/importer.go`
   - Refactor `getJSON` into `getRawJSON(ctx, endpoint, auth, query) ([]byte, error)` (read body once); callers do `json.Unmarshal(body, &typed)` then `collector.check(...)`. Imports are network-bound; the second parse of a sampled prefix is negligible.
   - `run()`: create a `driftCollector`; thread it into `fetchLookups`/`importDay`. On each NEW finding, `log("warn", "schema drift: <endpoint>.<field> <kind> (<detail>)")` so it appears live in LogTerminal via the existing log event path.
   - Hard-fail semantics: `missing_required` on heartbeats => skip that day's insert (record error-severity finding + error log line, continue — matches existing per-day resilience). `missing_required` on user_agents/machine_names (`id`/`value`) or missing `data` envelope => fail the job (already the behavior when fetchLookups errors). Everything else warns and proceeds.
   - Before `FinishImportJob` (all terminal paths incl. cancelled), persist findings: `w.DB.SetJobDrift(ctx, jobID, findingsJSON)`. Also persist after fetchLookups drift so early-failing jobs carry findings.
   - Testability: make the base URL injectable (`Worker.BaseURL` defaulting to the `wakatimeAPI` const) so tests can point at httptest.
3. `internal/db` — persistence + exposure
   - Migration `internal/db/migrations/00014_import_jobs_drift.sql`: `ALTER TABLE import_jobs ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS drift JSONB;` (nullable; Down drops it).
   - `internal/db/importjobs.go`: add `Drift json.RawMessage \`json:"drift"\`` to `db.Job` (RawMessage avoids db->importer import cycle; FE just sees the array). Add `drift` to `jobColumns` + `scanJob`. New method `SetJobDrift(ctx, id int, drift []byte) error`.
   - No handler changes needed for exposure: `db.Job` is already serialized in `/import/jobs`, `/import/jobs/:id`, and every WS snapshot/progress/state event — the `drift` field rides along automatically, including for historical runs.

## Frontend changes

1. `web/src/types/api.ts`: add `DriftFinding` interface (`endpoint`, `kind`, `field`, `detail`, `severity`, `firstSeenDay`, `count`) and `drift: DriftFinding[] | null` on `ImportJob`.
2. New `web/src/components/import/DriftBanner.tsx`: amber warning banner (red-tinted when any finding has `severity === "error"`), headline like "wakatime.com API drift detected — 3 finding(s); some fields may not have been imported", with a collapsible details table: endpoint / field / kind / detail / count / first seen. Follow the Card + lucide-icon style used by `CurrentRunPanel` (e.g. `TriangleAlert` icon).
3. Wire-up:
   - `web/src/components/import/CurrentRunPanel.tsx`: render `<DriftBanner findings={job.drift}/>` when non-empty — updates live because progress/state events carry the fresh Job.
   - `web/src/components/import/RunDetail.tsx`: same banner for historical runs.
   - `web/src/components/import/HistoryList.tsx` (optional polish): small warning icon on rows whose job has drift.
   - No changes needed in `useImportJobSocket.ts` (it stores whole Job objects).

## Drift vs hard-fail summary

- WARN (import proceeds): unknown field not in struct tags nor baseline allowlist; type change on an optional field (that field imports as nil for affected rows); new envelope keys.
- ERROR, skip day, job continues: required heartbeat field (`entity`/`type`/`time`/`user_agent_id`) missing or type-changed — inserting would mangle data.
- ERROR, fail job: `data` envelope missing/non-array, or user_agents/machine_names lacking `id`/`value` (every heartbeat's UA resolution would silently break).

## Testing strategy

- Pure unit tests in `internal/importer/drift_test.go` against fixture JSON strings: (a) golden current-schema payload incl. baseline unmapped fields => zero findings; (b) injected unknown field => one `unknown_field` warning; (c) removed `entity` => `missing_required` error; (d) `time` as string => `type_changed`; (e) dedupe/count across repeated items; (f) envelope without `data`.
- Integration test with `httptest.Server` + injectable BaseURL and the existing test DB harness (see `internal/db/importjobs_test.go`, `internal/testutil`): run a 1-day job against drifted fixtures, assert `import_jobs.drift` persisted, warn log line emitted, and job still `completed`; second test asserting required-lookup drift fails the job.
- FE: type-check only unless component tests exist; manual verification of banner via a seeded job row.

## Risks & gotchas

- False-positive noise is the #1 risk: wakatime ships benign additive fields regularly. Mitigations: baseline allowlist seeded from a real capture, dedupe-with-count, warning (not error) severity, and treating envelope extras leniently.
- Optional-field ABSENCE is not drift (JSON omits null fields routinely) — only required fields trigger `missing_required`.
- `dependencies` heartbeats field is `[]string`; a per-element type change won't fail whole-array decode cleanly — the type check should treat "known field fails typed decode" uniformly.
- WS payload growth: drift array is capped naturally by dedupe (bounded by field count); still cap findings at e.g. 50.
- Cancelled/failed paths must also persist drift (use the background-context pattern already in `finishCancelled`).

## Open questions for DJ

1. Baseline allowlist: can you capture one real response each for heartbeats/user_agents/machine_names from your wakatime account to seed the known-unmapped field lists? (Plan assumes at least `id`, `created_at` on heartbeats.)
2. Should a completed-with-drift job get a distinct visual state in HistoryList (badge variant) or is the banner in RunDetail enough?
3. Cover `all_time_since_today` (range discovery) too, or only the three import endpoints? Plan says optional/cheap to include.
4. Sample size per day (default 5 items) and findings cap (default 50) — any preference?
5. Any desire to suppress previously-acknowledged drift across future jobs (persistent "known drift" table), or is per-job reporting fine for v1? Plan assumes per-job.

## Critical files

- /Users/hdaniels/catalyst-devspace/repos/gakatime/internal/importer/importer.go
- /Users/hdaniels/catalyst-devspace/repos/gakatime/internal/db/importjobs.go
- /Users/hdaniels/catalyst-devspace/repos/gakatime/internal/db/migrations/00014_import_jobs_drift.sql (new)
- /Users/hdaniels/catalyst-devspace/repos/gakatime/web/src/types/api.ts
- /Users/hdaniels/catalyst-devspace/repos/gakatime/web/src/components/import/CurrentRunPanel.tsx

design:
# Plan: Save/Load entire DB to/from a file (Heartbeats page)

## Approach

Add two authenticated endpoints — a streaming full-logical-dump download and an upload+replace restore — plus "Save DB" / "Load DB" controls on the Heartbeats page next to the derived-data panel.

**Export format decision: ZIP archive of per-table Postgres COPY text files + a JSON manifest, produced Go-natively via pgx.**

- File: `boomtime-backup-<ts>.zip` containing:
  - `manifest.json` — `{ app: "boomtime", formatVersion: 1, createdAt, gooseVersion, tables: [{name, columns, rows}] }`. `gooseVersion` = `SELECT max(version_id) FROM goose_db_version`; per-table `columns` recorded to enable future forward-compat.
  - `tables/<name>.copy` — output of `COPY <table> (col, ...) TO STDOUT` (Postgres text format: exact round-trip of NULLs, arrays, bytea, timestamps).
- Why Go-native COPY instead of pg_dump: no pg_dump binary in the alpine app image (ticket constraint), and pgx v5 (already at v5.10.0 in go.mod) exposes `conn.PgConn().CopyTo(ctx, w, sql)` / `CopyFrom(ctx, r, sql)` — raw COPY protocol streaming with no row-by-row marshalling.
- Why ZIP not tar.gz: `archive/tar` requires entry sizes up-front (would force spooling each table to temp files); `archive/zip`'s writer streams entries without knowing sizes (data descriptors), so the dump can stream straight to the HTTP response. On restore we spool the upload to a temp file anyway (needed for `zip.NewReader`'s ReaderAt, and it lets us fully validate the archive+manifest *before* truncating anything). Deflate gives good compression on COPY text.
- Snapshot consistency: run the whole dump on one acquired conn inside `BEGIN ISOLATION LEVEL REPEATABLE READ READ ONLY` so all tables come from a single MVCC snapshot.

**Table inventory** (from migrations 00001–00013; `tags`/`project_tags` were dropped in 00013 and are excluded). FK-safe load order — this exact order is the dump order too:

1. `users` (PK username)
2. `projects` (FK → users)
3. `auth_tokens` (FK → users)
4. `refresh_tokens` (FK → users)
5. `heartbeats` (FK → users, → projects(owner,name))
6. `badges` (FK → users, → projects)
7. `hb_rollup_daily` (no FK)
8. `curation_rules` (no FK)
9. `spaces` (no FK enforced; owner column)
10. `space_rules` (FK → spaces, ON DELETE CASCADE)
11. `import_jobs` (no FK)
12. `import_job_logs` (FK → import_jobs)

`goose_db_version` is NOT restored (schema is owned by the running binary's migrations); its max version is recorded in the manifest and checked on restore.

## Backend changes

**New file `internal/db/dump.go`** (package db, alongside `derived.go`):
- `var dumpTables = []dumpTable{ {name, columns []string}, ... }` in the FK-safe order above. Explicit column lists on both COPY directions guard against column-order drift.
- `func (d *DB) DumpAll(ctx context.Context, w io.Writer) error` — acquire conn from `d.Pool`, open repeatable-read read-only tx, write `manifest.json` then each `tables/<n>.copy` into a `zip.Writer` via `PgConn().CopyTo(ctx, zw, "COPY <t> (cols) TO STDOUT")`. Row counts for the manifest come from `CopyTo`'s CommandTag (`RowsAffected()`); since zip entries stream, write the manifest LAST (or as the final entry `manifest.json`) so counts are known — reader locates it by name, order in archive is irrelevant.
- `func (d *DB) RestoreAll(ctx context.Context, zr *zip.Reader) (RestoreSummary, error)` — single tx on one acquired conn:
  1. Validate manifest (app id, formatVersion == 1, gooseVersion == current DB's max version_id — mismatch is a 409-style error, nothing touched).
  2. `TRUNCATE users, projects, auth_tokens, refresh_tokens, heartbeats, badges, hb_rollup_daily, curation_rules, spaces, space_rules, import_jobs, import_job_logs RESTART IDENTITY CASCADE` (one statement).
  3. For each table in FK order: `PgConn().CopyFrom(ctx, entryReader, "COPY <t> (cols) FROM STDIN")`.
  4. Reset sequences for every serial PK (`heartbeats.id`, `curation_rules.id`, `spaces.id`, `space_rules.id`, `import_jobs.id`, `import_job_logs.id`): `SELECT setval(pg_get_serial_sequence('<t>','id'), coalesce(max(id),1), max(id) IS NOT NULL) FROM <t>`.
  5. Normalize interrupted job states (`UPDATE import_jobs SET state='failed' WHERE state IN ('queued','running')`) — mirrors `importer.Worker.RecoverInterrupted`.
  6. COMMIT.
- `RestoreSummary` struct: per-table row counts + gooseVersion, JSON-tagged like `DerivedStatus`.

**Derived resync after restore** (in handler, after commit): the dump already contains `gap_seconds` + `hb_rollup_daily`, but per ticket run a safety-net rebuild: `SELECT DISTINCT sender FROM heartbeats` → for each, existing `d.ResyncDerived(ctx, sender)` (`internal/db/derived.go:51`, which calls `RecomputeGaps` + `RefreshRollup` from epoch). Then `h.Cache.InvalidatePrefix("")` to drop ALL cached aggregates (every key starts with `owner|`, so empty prefix clears everything).

**New file `internal/handler/backup.go`** (mirrors `derived.go` style):
- `GET /api/v1/users/current/db/export` → `h.DBExport`: `resolveUser` auth; set `Content-Type: application/zip` + `Content-Disposition: attachment; filename=boomtime-backup-<ts>.zip`; stream `h.DB.DumpAll(ctx, c.Response())`.
- `POST /api/v1/users/current/db/import?confirm=replace-all-data` → `h.DBImport`: `resolveUser` auth; require the literal `confirm` param server-side (400 without it — prevents accidental curl); reject 409 if an import job is running (check via Worker/import_jobs state); spool `c.Request().Body` to a temp file with `io.Copy` + `http.MaxBytesReader` size cap (env-tunable, e.g. `BOOM_RESTORE_MAX_BYTES`, default ~4GB); `zip.NewReader` + manifest validation; `h.DB.RestoreAll`; resync derived; invalidate cache; return `RestoreSummary` JSON. Guard with a package-level `sync.Mutex` (or atomic flag) on Handler so only one export/restore runs at a time.
- Content type: raw `application/zip` body (not multipart) — simplest streaming, one file, matches fetch upload.

**Route registration** in `internal/server/server.go` next to the derived routes (lines ~84–85):
```
e.GET("/api/v1/users/current/db/export", h.DBExport)
e.POST("/api/v1/users/current/db/import", h.DBImport)
```

## Frontend changes

- **`web/src/lib/api.ts`**: `exportDb(): Promise<Blob>` and `importDb(file: File): Promise<RestoreSummary>`. The shared `request()` JSON-parses, so both use direct `fetch` with `authStore.authHeader()` + `credentials: "include"`; export reads `res.blob()`, import POSTs the File as the raw body with `?confirm=replace-all-data`.
- **`web/src/types/api.ts`**: add `RestoreSummary` next to `DerivedStatus` (line 45).
- **New `web/src/components/heartbeats/BackupPanel.tsx`** rendered in `web/src/pages/Heartbeats.tsx` directly below `<DerivedStatusPanel />` (the derived-data panel, per ticket). Contents:
  - "Save DB" button: `useMutation(api.exportDb)` → `URL.createObjectURL(blob)` + programmatic `<a download>` click + revoke. Spinner while pending; sonner toast on error (matches `DerivedStatusPanel` patterns).
  - "Load DB" button (`variant="destructive"` outline): hidden `<input type="file" accept=".zip,application/zip">`; selecting a file opens the confirm dialog.
- **New `web/src/components/heartbeats/RestoreConfirmDialog.tsx`** using `@/components/ui/dialog` (pattern: `RenameGroupDialog.tsx`): shows file name + size, red warning copy ("This ERASES every table — heartbeats, projects, users, tokens, settings — and replaces them with the backup. You will likely be logged out."), a text input requiring the user to type `REPLACE` before the destructive submit enables, and a pending state during upload/restore.
  - On success: toast with restored row counts, `queryClient.clear()` (all data changed), then call `api.getCurrentUser()`; on 401/403 (the restored `auth_tokens`/`refresh_tokens` don't contain the current session) clear auth state and redirect to `/login` via `useAuth`.

## Security / guarding

- Both endpoints behind `resolveUser` token auth like every other handler; single-user self-host, but note the export is inherently whole-DB (contains ALL users' data + password hashes + auth/refresh tokens) — call this out in the modal and keep the filename obviously sensitive.
- Restore triple-guarded: (1) server-side `confirm=replace-all-data` param, (2) client typed-`REPLACE` modal, (3) single-flight mutex + running-import-job rejection.
- Full validation (zip integrity via central directory, manifest app/format/goose version) happens on the spooled temp file BEFORE the TRUNCATE — a corrupt/foreign file can never wipe data. Restore itself is one transaction: any COPY error rolls back to the pre-restore state.
- Upload size cap via `http.MaxBytesReader`; temp file created in `os.TempDir()` and removed via defer.

## Testing strategy

- **`internal/db/dump_test.go`** (uses `harness_test.go` fixtures + isolated test DB): seed users/projects/heartbeats/curation/spaces/tokens/import jobs → `DumpAll` to buffer → mutate+add rows → `RestoreAll` → assert exact row equality per table, sequence positions (insert a new heartbeat post-restore, no PK collision), `gap_seconds`/rollup intact, running jobs normalized to failed. Plus: manifest gooseVersion mismatch rejected with nothing truncated; corrupt zip rejected.
- **`internal/handler/backup_test.go`** (pattern: `curation_test.go` via testutil/dbtest): 400 without confirm param, 401/403 without token, happy-path round trip through HTTP, cache invalidated.
- **Frontend (vitest, patterns in `api.test.ts` / `SourceHealthPanel.test.tsx`)**: confirm button disabled until `REPLACE` typed; api functions hit right URLs with auth header; post-success logout path on 401.
- **Manual e2e** via docker-compose: dump ~440k-heartbeat DB, restore into a freshly-migrated empty DB, verify Overview/Heartbeats dashboards and login with backed-up credentials.

## Risks & gotchas

- **Session self-invalidation**: restore replaces `auth_tokens`/`refresh_tokens`; when loading another instance's backup the caller's token vanishes mid-session. Handled by post-restore auth re-check + redirect to login (see frontend). Don't try to re-insert the caller's token — its user may not exist in the backup.
- **Schema drift**: a dump from an older/newer binary has different columns. v1 requires exact gooseVersion match; manifest records columns so a later version can do tolerant column-intersection restores.
- **Memory**: dump streams end-to-end (COPY → zip → response). Browser-side `res.blob()` holds the file in RAM — fine at expected sizes (~440k heartbeats ≈ tens of MB deflated); if backups grow huge, switch Save DB to a native `<a href>` download using the existing `?token=` query-auth pattern (`resolveUserFromQueryToken`, handler.go:114).
- **TRUNCATE locks**: restore takes ACCESS EXCLUSIVE on all tables; concurrent dashboard queries will briefly block — acceptable for single-user, and the mutex prevents concurrent restores.
- **`heartbeats_sender_project_fkey`**: projects MUST load before heartbeats (order above); a dump is internally consistent so no orphan risk.
- **Serial sequences**: forgetting `setval` after COPY of explicit ids ⇒ duplicate-PK failures on the next ingest. Covered by step 4 + test.
- **fetch has no upload progress**; acceptable v1 (spinner). XHR-based progress is a possible follow-up.

## Open questions for DJ

1. Strict gooseVersion equality on restore, or accept older dumps (restore column-intersection then rely on migrations having already run)? Plan assumes strict-equal for v1.
2. Is forced re-login after restore acceptable UX? (Alternative is messy.)
3. ZIP chosen over tar.gz for Go streaming ergonomics — any preference for tar.gz for tooling reasons (e.g. inspecting with standard unix tools)?
4. Want a matching CLI subcommand (`boomtime dump` / `boomtime restore` in cmd/boomtime/main.go) reusing `DumpAll`/`RestoreAll` for cron/scripted backups? Cheap once the db layer exists.
5. Cap for upload size — is a 4GB default sensible for your data volumes?
6. Should `badges` (has data? feature looks dormant) and `import_job_logs` be included? Plan includes both for true full-fidelity.

### Critical Files for Implementation
- /Users/hdaniels/catalyst-devspace/repos/gakatime/internal/db/dump.go (new; model on internal/db/derived.go + sessions.go RecomputeGaps/RefreshRollup)
- /Users/hdaniels/catalyst-devspace/repos/gakatime/internal/handler/backup.go (new; model on internal/handler/derived.go)
- /Users/hdaniels/catalyst-devspace/repos/gakatime/internal/server/server.go (route registration, ~line 84)
- /Users/hdaniels/catalyst-devspace/repos/gakatime/web/src/components/heartbeats/BackupPanel.tsx (new; model on DerivedStatusPanel.tsx; mount in web/src/pages/Heartbeats.tsx)
- /Users/hdaniels/catalyst-devspace/repos/gakatime/web/src/lib/api.ts (exportDb/importDb + types in web/src/types/api.ts)

title: Charts with an 'Other' group: tooltip shows the Other breakdown
description: Every viz that collapses the long tail into a generic 'Other (N more)' bucket (top-N + Other — column/pie/heatmaps/momentum/etc.) should, on hovering the Other segment, show a tooltip breaking down what's inside it (the individual members + their values/%), not just 'Other'. Requires the payload to carry (or the FE to retain) the collapsed members, or a drill query. Audit every top-N+Other chart and add the breakdown tooltip.
design:
# gaka-7m4 — "Other" bucket breakdown tooltips: implementation plan

Scope: ONLY the mechanism for showing what's inside an "Other (N more)" bucket on hover. Shared tooltip styling/richness is owned by sibling gaka-9pt; this plan adds the minimal data plumbing + a small breakdown-HTML helper that gaka-9pt can later restyle.

## 1. Audit — where "Other" comes from and which charts show it

Collapsing happens ONLY in the backend, in `capWithOther()` at `/Users/hdaniels/catalyst-devspace/repos/gakatime/internal/stats/stats.go` (~line 395, `resourceTopN = 12`). It keeps top-12 `model.ResourceStats` and synthesizes one `Other (N more)` entry with summed `TotalSeconds/TotalPct/TotalDaily/PctDaily` — the tail members are DROPPED, so the FE cannot recover them today. Call sites:
- Stats payload (`/api/v1/users/current/stats`): projects, editors, languages, platforms, machines, categories (stats.go ~329-334)
- Project statistics: languages (~555, reused for `LanguagesDaily` via `languagesDaily()` ~595), files (~574), branches (~639)
- Momentum endpoint (`internal/stats/bigbets.go`) does NOT create an Other bucket (top-N only) — despite the ticket text, MomentumGrid is NOT affected.

Charts that RENDER an Other segment (affected — need the breakdown tooltip):
1. `web/src/components/charts/PieChart.tsx` — renders every item incl. Other. Used by Overview "Project breakdown" (`stats.projects`) and Projects "Language breakdown" (`stats.languages`).
2. `web/src/components/charts/ColumnChart.tsx` (stacked mode) — Overview "Total activity" stacked by category (incl. Other, built in `OverviewDashboard.tsx` `categoryColumnSeries`), and Projects "Total activity" stacked by language (built from `languagesDaily` in `web/src/pages/Projects.tsx` `languageColumnSeries`, which includes the "Other (N more)" series).
3. `web/src/components/charts/HeatmapChart.tsx` — Overview "Activity per project" + "Activity per language"; explicitly keeps the Other row (isOther logic at ~line 31).
4. `web/src/viz/council/CategoryStreamgraph.tsx` — keeps the Other layer last (~line 38).

Charts that deliberately EXCLUDE Other (no change; document as audited):
- `web/src/viz/council/CategoryBreakdown.tsx` (filters it out entirely — see open question)
- `web/src/components/TopProjectsBar.tsx`, `web/src/viz/council/BranchActivity.tsx`, `web/src/components/charts/FileBarChart.tsx`, `web/src/lib/mostActive.ts` (StatCards)
- `web/src/viz/council/MomentumGrid.tsx` (payload has no Other)
- editors/platforms/machines lists are not consumed by any FE chart today.

## 2. Approach (recommended mechanism)

**Backend carries the collapsed members on the Other entry itself** (Option A). Rejected alternatives: a drill query (per-hover latency, new endpoint, cache keys — overkill for a tooltip) and FE retention (impossible — the FE never sees the tail).

- Extend `model.ResourceStats` (`/Users/hdaniels/catalyst-devspace/repos/gakatime/internal/model/model.go` ~line 81) with:
  `OtherMembers []OtherMember \`json:"otherMembers,omitempty"\`` and a new
  `OtherMember { Name string \`json:"name"\`; TotalSeconds int64 \`json:"totalSeconds"\`; TotalPct float64 \`json:"totalPct"\` }`.
  Only the synthesized Other entry populates it; `omitempty` keeps every existing payload byte-identical otherwise.
- In `capWithOther()`: the tail is already sorted desc (list is sorted before slicing); carry the top `otherMembersCap = 20` tail members (name/totalSeconds/totalPct only — NO per-day arrays, that's exactly what capping exists to avoid). The FE derives "…and K more" as `tailCount - members.length`, where tailCount parses from the existing `Other (N more)` name (or add an explicit `otherCount int` field — cleaner; see open questions).
- Range-total breakdown only: day-scoped tooltips (stacked columns, heatmap cells) show the member breakdown *over the whole range*, labeled as such. Carrying per-day tail matrices would reinflate the payload capping exists to bound.

## 3. Shared changes

Backend:
- `internal/model/model.go`: `OtherMember` type + `ResourceStats.OtherMembers` (+ optional `OtherCount`).
- `internal/stats/stats.go`: populate members in `capWithOther`. `languagesDaily()` stays as-is (`LanguageDaily` keeps only name+daily; the FE joins back to the capped `Languages` list by name for members).

Frontend shared:
- `web/src/types/api.ts`: mirror `otherMembers?: { name: string; totalSeconds: number; totalPct: number }[]` (+ `otherCount?`) on `ResourceStats` (~line 80s).
- New `web/src/viz/otherBreakdown.ts`:
  - `isOtherBucket(name)` — consolidate the 6+ duplicated `startsWith("Other (")` checks (HeatmapChart, CategoryStreamgraph, CategoryBreakdown, OverviewDashboard, TopProjectsBar, mostActive) OR reuse/re-export the existing one in `web/src/lib/mostActive.ts`.
  - `otherBreakdownHtml(r: { otherMembers?, totalSeconds, name })` → HTML string: one row per member (truncated name via `truncate` from `@/lib/utils`, `secondsToHms`, pct-of-Other), display-capped at ~8 rows + a final `+K more (Xh Ym)` line. Returns "" when no members. Keep markup/inline styles consistent with existing `web/src/viz/d3/tooltip.ts` tooltips so gaka-9pt can restyle in one place.
- `web/src/components/charts/types.ts`: `ColumnSeries` gains optional `otherMembers` (or simpler: `stats?: ResourceStats` back-reference).

## 4. Per-chart checklist

- **PieChart** (`web/src/components/charts/PieChart.tsx`): in the `mousemove` handler (~line 61) append `otherBreakdownHtml(d.data)` when present. Data already flows (items are ResourceStats).
- **ColumnChart stacked** (`web/src/components/charts/ColumnChart.tsx` ~line 147): append breakdown from `ser.otherMembers`; label it e.g. "Top in Other (range)". Callers must thread members:
  - `web/src/components/OverviewDashboard.tsx` `categoryColumnSeries` (~line 154): pass `otherMembers: c.otherMembers` through (note `bucketItems` spread already preserves extra fields).
  - `web/src/pages/Projects.tsx` `languageColumnSeries` (~line 122): join `languagesDaily` name → `stats.languages` entry to attach `otherMembers` to the Other series.
- **HeatmapChart** (`web/src/components/charts/HeatmapChart.tsx` mousemove ~line 137): rows are ResourceStats, so `otherBreakdownHtml(d.row)` when hovering the Other row.
- **CategoryStreamgraph** (`web/src/viz/council/CategoryStreamgraph.tsx` mousemove ~line 107): it already does `series.find(s => s.name === layer.key)`; append breakdown from that entry.
- **Audited, no change**: CategoryBreakdown, TopProjectsBar, BranchActivity, FileBarChart, MomentumGrid, Punchcard/Calendar/Sessions (no Other buckets).

## 5. Testing

- Go: extend `internal/stats/capwithother_test.go` — members populated only on the Other entry, sorted desc, capped at `otherMembersCap`, empty when list ≤ topN; `internal/stats/project_extras_test.go` branch/files cases assert members too. `go test ./internal/stats/...`.
- FE unit (vitest, patterns in `web/src/viz/bucket.test.ts` / `web/src/lib/mostActive.test.ts`): `otherBreakdown.test.ts` — html rows, display cap + "+K more" math, empty-string when absent; `isOtherBucket` cases incl. plain "Other" category vs "Other (N more)".
- Manual: seed >12 projects/languages/categories; hover Other in the 4 affected charts (Overview + Projects pages, light/dark); confirm non-Other tooltips unchanged; confirm payload size stays sane on all-time range with many files.

## 6. Risks & gotchas

- **"Other" the real category vs "Other (N more)" the aggregate**: a genuine "Other" category value exists (see `mostActive.ts`). Gate the breakdown on `otherMembers` presence, never on name matching.
- **HeatmapChart FE re-slices to topN=7** (~line 36): rows 8–12 are silently dropped and NOT folded into the Other row, so the Other row's breakdown will list only the backend tail (items 13+), not the dropped 8–12. Pre-existing inconsistency — call out in tooltip copy or fix separately (open question).
- **Day-scoped tooltips show range totals**: label clearly ("in range") so a per-day stacked segment tooltip doesn't imply per-day member values.
- **Payload growth**: files tails can be huge — the 20-member cap bounds it (~1–2 KB per capped list). `omitempty` keeps Wakatime-compatible consumers unaffected.
- **Tooltip `white-space: nowrap`** in `web/src/viz/d3/tooltip.ts` — multi-row breakdown needs block rows + truncated long names (file paths!) or the tip overflows; coordinate with gaka-9pt before changing shared tooltip CSS.
- OverviewDashboard's `bucketItems` uses object spread, so `otherMembers` survives bucketing automatically — but verify no other reshaping strips it (e.g. `categoryColumnSeries` maps to a new object; must copy explicitly).

## 7. Open questions for DJ

1. Explicit `otherCount` field vs parsing "Other (N more)" from the name (name parsing breaks if the label is ever localized/renamed)? Recommend the field.
2. Member caps: 20 carried / 8 displayed OK? Files tails especially can be hundreds.
3. Should `CategoryBreakdown` (currently hides Other entirely) start rendering an Other row with the breakdown, or stay as-is?
4. Fix the HeatmapChart topN=7 re-slice so dropped rows fold into Other (accurate breakdown), or accept "backend tail only"?
5. Percentages in the tooltip: % of Other, % of chart total, or both?

title: Tooltip audit + re-up across all visualizations
description: Full audit of every chart/viz tooltip; make each richer + more relevant to the hovered entity + its visualization. Per viz, decide the most useful stats to surface on hover (e.g. value + %-of-total + rank + delta vs prior period + count/heartbeats + date range), consistent styling, and the 'Other' breakdown (see sibling bead). Deliver an audit doc (per-chart current vs proposed tooltip) then implement. Covers D3 viz-council charts + the base column/pie/heatmap/radar/timeline + punchcard/momentum/streamgraph/calendar.
design: # gaka-9pt — Tooltip audit + re-up across all visualizations ## Approach Every chart in the app already renders tooltips through one tiny shared helper (`web/src/viz/d3/tooltip.ts`: `createTooltip` / `showTooltip` / `hideTooltip`), but each chart hand-builds its HTML with ad-hoc string concatenation. Result: inconsistent structure (some have bold titles, some don't), thin content (usually just `name` + `secondsToHms`), unescaped user-controlled strings injected into `innerHTML` (project/branch/file names — a latent XSS), no edge-clamping (tooltips overflow the right/bottom of cards), and misleading date labels on bucketed series (a "weekly" bucket tooltips as a single day). Plan in three moves: 1. **Shared tooltip infrastructure**: a structured tooltip-content builder (title / rows / footer, colored swatches, delta arrows, HTML escaping) + smarter positioning in `tooltip.ts`. All charts migrate to it, giving consistent styling for free. 2. **Per-chart enrichment** (audit table below): each chart surfaces the most decision-useful stats it can compute from data it *already receives* — value, %-of-total, rank, per-bucket date ranges, deltas computable in-series (e.g. week-over-week in MomentumGrid). Almost everything is FE-only: `ResourceStats` already carries `totalPct`/`totalDaily`/`pctDaily`, and ranks/ranges are derivable client-side. 3. **Optional data adds** (flagged, small): prior-period delta via a second `getStats` fetch for the preceding window; heartbeat counts per resource (backend); per-bin `totalSeconds` in the sessions histogram (backend). The 'Other (N more)' member breakdown is **owned by sibling gaka-7m4** — not re-planned here. The shared builder's row-list API is deliberately shaped so gaka-7m4 can feed collapsed members (name + value + % rows with swatches and an overflow footer) into the same tooltip with zero new styling work. ## Shared tooltip infrastructure ### Files - `web/src/viz/d3/tooltip.ts` (extend) — keep `createTooltip`/`showTooltip`/`hideTooltip` API; add edge-aware positioning: clamp/flip so the tip never overflows the container (measure tip after `.html()`, flip left of cursor when `x + tipW > containerW`, above when `y + tipH > containerH`). Widen `padding` slightly and drop `white-space: nowrap` in favor of `max-width: 320px` so multi-row tooltips wrap sanely. - `web/src/viz/d3/tooltipContent.ts` (new) — pure functions, no DOM: - `escapeHtml(s: string): string` — used on every interpolated name. - `tooltipHtml(spec: TooltipSpec): string` where ```ts interface TooltipRow { label: string; value: string; swatch?: string; muted?: boolean } interface TooltipSpec { title: string; // bold first line (escaped) subtitle?: string; // muted second line, e.g. the date/date-range rows?: TooltipRow[]; // label→value grid lines footer?: string; // muted last line, e.g. "rank 3 of 14" or "+2 more" } ``` - Formatters: `fmtPct(x: number)` (1 dp, clamps), `fmtDelta(cur: number, prev: number)` → `▲ +1h 12m (+18%)` / `▼ −24m (−9%)` with `--success`/`--destructive` colored span, `fmtDateRange(startISO: string, endISO: string)` → `12–18 Jan 2026` (collapses when same day), `fmtRank(i: number, n: number)` → `#3 of 14`. - `web/src/viz/bucket.ts` (extend) — add `bucketRanges(groups: number[][], dates: string[]): { start: string; end: string }[]` so bucketed charts can tooltip the *range* a bar/cell covers instead of its first day. Mirror tests in `web/src/viz/bucket.test.ts`. - New test file `web/src/viz/d3/tooltipContent.test.ts`. ### Styling conventions (all charts) - Line 1: **entity** (bold, escaped, swatch dot when the chart is multi-series). - Line 2 (muted): temporal context — exact day or bucket date range. - Rows: `Time` (Hms) · `Share` (% of visible total) · chart-specific extras. - Footer (muted): rank / deltas / hints (e.g. "UTC"). ## Audit table — current vs proposed All paths under `/Users/hdaniels/catalyst-devspace/repos/gakatime/`. "Gap" = data not currently reaching the component. | # | Chart (file) | Current tooltip | Proposed tooltip | Data gaps | |---|---|---|---|---| | 1 | ColumnChart — `web/src/components/charts/ColumnChart.tsx` (Overview "Total activity" stacked by category; Projects stacked by language / flat) | Stacked: series name + `12 Jan: 1h 2m` (segment only). Flat: date + `Coding time: Hms` | Title: segment/series name (swatch). Subtitle: bucket **date range**. Rows: segment time, % of that bar's total, bar total (all segments). Flat: day/bucket time + % of range total + delta vs previous bucket (in-series). | None for values (series arrays already passed). Needs `bucketRanges` threaded via new optional prop `ranges?: {start,end}[]` on `ColumnChartProps` (`web/src/components/charts/types.ts`); callers `OverviewDashboard.tsx` / `Projects.tsx` pass it. | | 2 | PieChart — `web/src/components/charts/PieChart.tsx` (project + language breakdown) | name + Hms (slice ≥5% shows % label on-chart) | Title: name. Rows: time, **share %** (use `totalPct` or recompute over filtered set), **rank** `#i of N` (use `projectsCount`/`languagesCount` for true N), daily avg (totalSeconds / active days from `totalDaily`). 'Other' slice: member breakdown per **gaka-7m4**. | None — `ResourceStats` has everything; true counts need the `*Count` fields passed as an optional `totalCount?: number` prop. | | 3 | HeatmapChart — `web/src/components/charts/HeatmapChart.tsx` (activity per project / per language) | row name + `12 Jan: Hms` | Title: row name (swatch). Subtitle: bucket range. Rows: cell time, % of that row's range total, % of that **day/bucket's** all-rows total (column sum over `items`), row rank. | None; needs `ranges` prop like #1. | | 4 | RadarChart — `web/src/components/charts/RadarChart.tsx` (activity per weekday) | weekday + `Activity: Hms` | Title: weekday. Rows: total time, % of week total, **avg per occurrence** (total ÷ number of that weekday in the range), rank ("your #1 day"). | Weekday-occurrence count needs the range: pass `startDate`/`endDate` (already in `ProjectStatistics`) as new props, count occurrences FE-side. | | 5 | HourBarChart — `web/src/components/charts/HourBarChart.tsx` (activity per hour) | `13:00` + Activity Hms | Title: `13:00–14:00`. Rows: time, % of daily-rhythm total, rank (peak hour highlighted). Footer: clarify timezone (values pass through `addTimeOffset`). | None. | | 6 | FileBarChart — `web/src/components/charts/FileBarChart.tsx` (most active files, top 10) | full path + Activity Hms | Title: basename (`shortPath`); subtitle: full path (escaped, wrapped). Rows: time, % of project total (`totalPct`), rank of shown 10 + true `filesCount` footer (`#3 of 87 files`). | `filesCount` passed as optional prop from `Projects.tsx`. | | 7 | TimelineChart — `web/src/components/charts/TimelineChart.tsx` (recent timeline lanes) | lang + `12 Jan, 09:10 → 11:42` + duration | Keep; add rows: % of the lane's window total, lane total (`Xh across N segments today`). | None — computable from `segments`. | | 8 | ContributionCalendar — `web/src/viz/council/ContributionCalendar.tsx` | `Fri 09 Jan 2026` + Hms / "No activity" | Rows: time, **vs daily average** (`+38% vs avg`), rank among active days (`#5 of 212 active days`), intensity quartile. | None — full `values` array is in-component. | | 9 | CumulativeArea — `web/src/viz/council/CumulativeArea.tsx` | date + `Total: Hms` | Subtitle: bucket range. Rows: running total, **added this bucket** (delta from previous cum point), % of final total reached. | `ranges` prop (callers already compute buckets). | | 10 | CategoryStreamgraph — `web/src/viz/council/CategoryStreamgraph.tsx` | layer name + **range total** (same regardless of x-position — misleading) | Invert `x` from pointer → bucket index: Title: category (swatch). Subtitle: bucket range. Rows: category time **in that bucket**, % of that bucket across categories, range total for the category. | None — `totalDaily` per category is already passed; just use `x.invert` + `Math.round`. `ranges` prop for the subtitle. | | 11 | Punchcard — `web/src/viz/council/Punchcard.tsx` | `Tue 14:00 UTC` + Hms | Rows: cell time, % of `totalSeconds` (already in payload), cell rank (`#2 busiest slot`). Footer: "UTC" note stays. | None. | | 12 | MomentumGrid — `web/src/viz/council/MomentumGrid.tsx` | project + `12 Jan 2026: Hms` | Title: project. Subtitle: **week range** (Mon–Sun from `weeks[wi]`). Rows: week time, **Δ vs previous week** (`fmtDelta` — this IS the momentum story), % of project's range total (`totalSeconds` in payload), project's rank that week (column comparison). | None — all from `MomentumPayload.weekly`. | | 13 | DeepWorkSessions histogram — `web/src/viz/council/DeepWorkSessions.tsx` | bin label + `N sessions` | Rows: count, % of all sessions (`summary.count`). Optional: total time in bin. | Time-in-bin needs backend: add `totalSeconds` to `SessionsHistogramBin` (`internal/db/sessions.go` or `internal/stats`, `internal/model/model.go`, FE `types/api.ts`). Ship % first, backend add optional. | | 14 | DeepWorkSessions daily strip — same file | date + `N sessions` | Subtitle: bucket range. Rows: sessions, **total focus time**, longest session — `SessionsDaily` already has `totalSeconds` + `longestSeconds`; bucket them alongside `sessions` (sum / sum / max). | None — data already in payload, currently dropped during bucketing. | | 15 | AuthoringVsReading — `web/src/viz/council/AuthoringVsReading.tsx` | Donut: label + Hms + % (best-in-class today). Ratio line: **no tooltip at all** | Donut: keep, add footer `of Xh tracked`. Line: add hover dots/overlay → date + authoring ratio %, and approx write/read split if per-bucket seconds passed. | Pass the page's bucketed `seconds` series (Projects.tsx already computes it) to scale ratio→time; exact per-bucket write seconds would need a backend `dailyWriteSeconds` — flag as optional. | | 16 | BranchActivity — `web/src/viz/council/BranchActivity.tsx` | branch + Hms | Rows: time, % of project total, rank; footer: `of N branches` (`branchesCount`). | `branchesCount` + project `totalSeconds` as props from `Projects.tsx`. | | 17 | BreadthVsDepth — `web/src/viz/council/BreadthVsDepth.tsx` | date + `Time: Hms` + `Files: N` | Subtitle: bucket range. Rows: time, files, **minutes/file** (depth signal), Δ vs previous bucket. | `ranges` prop. | | 18 | CategoryBreakdown — `web/src/viz/council/CategoryBreakdown.tsx` (HTML bars) | none (inline time + % already shown) | No hover tooltip needed (data already inline). Only if DJ wants it (open question 3). 3. Prior-period deltas need **no** backend change — FE fetches `getStats` for the preceding equal-length window (react-query cached) and joins by resource name. ## Implementation order - **Batch 1 — infra** (no visual change): `tooltipContent.ts` + tests; `tooltip.ts` clamping + max-width; `bucket.ts` `bucketRanges` + tests. Migrate ONE chart (PieChart) end-to-end as the reference. - **Batch 2 — base charts**: ColumnChart, HeatmapChart, RadarChart, HourBarChart, FileBarChart, TimelineChart (+ `types.ts` prop additions, `OverviewDashboard.tsx` / `Projects.tsx` callers pass `ranges`/counts). - **Batch 3 — council charts**: CategoryStreamgraph at-point fix (biggest single win), MomentumGrid WoW delta, ContributionCalendar, CumulativeArea, Punchcard, DeepWorkSessions (incl. keeping `totalSeconds`/`longestSeconds` through bucketing), AuthoringVsReading line hover, BranchActivity, BreadthVsDepth. - **Batch 4 — prior-period delta** (Overview + Projects): extra `getStats` prior-window query; deltas in Pie/Column tooltips. title: Changelog tab + release/build/versioning system (GHCR, no baked secrets)
description: (1) CHANGELOG generated from conventional-commit history (git-cliff + cliff.toml) into CHANGELOG.md, embedded (go:embed) and served at an endpoint; a 'Changelog' sidebar tab + page renders it grouped by version/type; show the running app version. (2) Versioning via git tags + ldflags (-X main.version=$(git describe --tags)) injected in the Dockerfile + Taskfile build; expose via /api/.../version. (3) Task commands: version, changelog, release (bump+changelog+tag), docker:build (version-tagged), docker:push to ghcr.io. (4) GHCR: image ghcr.io/<owner>/<app>; optional GH Actions release-on-tag workflow to build+push. (5) SECRETS SAFETY: audit the multi-stage Dockerfile (it COPYs '. .' — ensure no secrets baked); harden .dockerignore (currently missing .envrc, .env.* variants, *.local.json, .beads-credential-key) so nothing secret enters the build context; keep all HAKA_/BOOM_ secrets as RUNTIME env, never build args. NOTE: app name depends on gaka rename.
design: # Implementation Plan — Changelog tab + release/build/versioning system (GHCR, no baked secrets) ## Ground truth from exploration (2026-07-10) - App binary/module is **boomtime** (`cmd/boomtime`, module `github.com/TheBranchDriftCatalyst/boomtime`) — the gakatime→boomtime rename already landed (commit `05f3639 refactor!: rename application gakatime -> boomtime`). Local dir is still `gakatime`; git remote is still `git@github.com:TheBranchDriftCatalyst/hakatime.git`. Naming is unresolved (see Open Questions). - **No git tags exist** (`git describe --tags` fails: "No names found"). Commit history already follows conventional commits (feat/fix/chore/refactor!) — git-cliff will work well. - Router: `internal/server/server.go` (`registerRoutes`), handlers in `internal/handler/` (`handler.New(database, cfg, logger, worker, hub)`). SPA embedded via `//go:embed dist` in `internal/server/server.go`; `task build` copies `web/dist` → `internal/server/dist` (Dockerfile does the same via `COPY --from=web`). - Frontend: routes in `web/src/App.tsx` (children of `/app`), sidebar `NAV` array in `web/src/components/AppShell.tsx` (lucide icons), typed API client `web/src/lib/api.ts` (`request` already falls back to raw text when body isn't JSON — can fetch raw markdown as-is). Pages live in `web/src/pages/`. No markdown lib in `web/package.json`. - Dockerfile: 3-stage (node → golang `COPY . .` → alpine runtime). Final image only copies the binary, but secrets in the builder stage still land in layer/build-cache (leakable via `--target server`, buildx cache export). Build currently has NO version injection: `-ldflags="-s -w"` only. - **Sensitive/junk files actually present in repo root that the current build context picks up** (current `.dockerignore` only excludes: `.git`, `tmp/`, `bin/`, `.env`, `.env.local`, `.direnv/`, `web/node_modules/`, `web/dist/`, `web/.vite/`, `internal/server/dist/`, `.dolt/`, `.beads/`, `*.db`, `.DS_Store`): - `.envrc` (NOT ignored — enters context) - `.claude/settings.local.json` (NOT ignored — `*.local.json` pattern missing) - `.claude/`, `.serena/`, `.agents/`, `.codex/`, `.playwright-mcp/` dirs (NOT ignored) - `.beads-credential-key` — not currently on disk but anticipated by `.gitignore`; must be dockerignored proactively - `internal/db/testdata/*.local.json` (fixturegen `--raw` output with REAL values) — pattern missing - ~600KB of `*.png` screenshots, `ov.md`, `docs/` — bloat, not secret - `.env` itself IS already excluded (good); root `.env` on disk contains `BOOM_DB_PASS`, `WAKATIME_API_KEY`, `GITHUB_TOKEN` — confirms stakes. - All runtime config is `BOOM_*` env (see `.env.example`, `internal/config/config.go`); nothing currently uses build args — keep it that way for secrets. --- ## Workstream 1 — CHANGELOG generation + embed + endpoint + UI ### 1a. git-cliff config — new file `cliff.toml` (repo root) Sketch: ```toml [changelog] header = "# Changelog\n\n" body = """ ## [{{ version | trim_start_matches(pat="v") }}]{% if version %} - {{ timestamp | date(format="%Y-%m-%d") }}{% else %} unreleased{% endif %} {% for group, commits in commits | group_by(attribute="group") %} ### {{ group }} {% for commit in commits %}- {% if commit.scope %}**{{ commit.scope }}:** {% endif %}{{ commit.message }} {% endfor %}{% endfor %} """ trim = true [git] conventional_commits = true filter_unconventional = true tag_pattern = "v[0-9].*" sort_commits = "newest" commit_parsers = [ { message = "^feat", group = "Features" }, { message = "^fix", group = "Bug Fixes" }, { message = "^perf", group = "Performance" }, { message = "^refactor", group = "Refactoring" }, { message = "^docs", group = "Documentation" }, { message = "^chore\\(beads\\)", skip = true }, { message = "^chore\\(release\\)", skip = true }, { message = "^chore", group = "Chores" }, { body = ".*BREAKING", group = "Breaking Changes" }, ] ``` Generate and **commit** `CHANGELOG.md` at repo root (must be committed: `go:embed` fails to compile if the file is absent — this is the biggest gotcha). ### 1b. Embed + endpoints (Go) - New root-level `embed.go` (package importable as the module root, e.g. `package boomtime`): ```go //go:embed CHANGELOG.md var ChangelogMD []byte ``` (Root embed avoids the copy-into-package dance used for `web/dist`; CHANGELOG.md lives at root anyway. Alternative: `internal/changelog/` + a Taskfile copy step mirroring the dist pattern — root embed is simpler and stays fresh.) - `internal/config/config.go`: add `Version string` field (not env-loaded; set by main). - `cmd/boomtime/main.go`: `var version = "dev"` (package-level, ldflags target `main.version`); set `root.Version = version` (free `boomtime --version` via cobra) and `cfg.Version = version` after `config.Load()`. - New `internal/handler/meta.go` (or `version.go`): - `GET /api/v1/version` → `{"version": "...", }` (JSON) - `GET /api/v1/changelog` → raw markdown, `Content-Type: text/markdown; charset=utf-8` Register both in `registerRoutes` in `internal/server/server.go`. Decision: leave them unauthenticated (like badges) — version disclosure is low-risk on a self-hosted app; flip to session-auth if DJ objects. ### 1c. Frontend - `web/src/lib/api.ts`: add `api.version(): Promise` and `api.changelog(): Promise` (existing `request` returns raw text when JSON.parse fails — works for markdown). Add `VersionResponse` to `web/src/types/api.ts`. - New `web/src/pages/Changelog.tsx`: - Fetch markdown via react-query; parse with a **small custom parser** keyed to the deterministic git-cliff shape (`## [x.y.z] - date` / `### Group` / `- item`) → render grouped cards per version with per-type sections and badges. Avoids adding react-markdown/remark deps; the format is under our control via `cliff.toml`. (Fallback option if parsing feels brittle: `react-markdown` + `remark-gfm`.) - Header shows running version from `/api/v1/version`; highlight/pin the section matching the running version; mark newer sections "unreleased/newer than running". - Parser goes in `web/src/lib/changelog.ts` + `changelog.test.ts` (matches existing lib/test convention). - `web/src/App.tsx`: add `} />` under `/app`. - `web/src/components/AppShell.tsx`: add to `NAV`: `{ name: "Changelog", icon: History, to: "/app/changelog", end: false }` (lucide `History` or `Sparkles`). Optionally render the version string (from `api.version()`) as small text at the sidebar footer. ## Workstream 2 — Versioning via tags + ldflags - Version string: `git describe --tags --always --dirty` (`--always` is required today since no tags exist → yields short SHA until v0.1.0 is tagged). - `Dockerfile` (stage 2): add `ARG VERSION=dev` and change build line to: `RUN CGO_ENABLED=0 GOOS=linux go build -ldflags="-s -w -X main.version=${VERSION}" -o /out/boomtime ./cmd/boomtime` VERSION is not a secret → build-arg is fine. `.git` stays dockerignored; version is computed on the host/CI and passed in. - Optional: `LABEL org.opencontainers.image.version=${VERSION} org.opencontainers.image.source=https://github.com//` on the runtime stage (source label makes GHCR link the package to the repo). - `Taskfile.yml`: dynamic var ```yaml vars: VERSION: sh: git describe --tags --always --dirty ``` and `build:` gains `-ldflags "-X main.version={{.VERSION}}"`. ## Workstream 3 — Task commands (Taskfile.yml) ```yaml version: desc: Print the version that builds would embed cmds: ['echo "{{.VERSION}}"'] changelog: desc: Regenerate CHANGELOG.md from conventional commits (requires git-cliff) cmds: - 'command -v git-cliff >/dev/null || { echo "install git-cliff: brew install git-cliff"; exit 1; }' - git cliff -o CHANGELOG.md release: desc: 'Bump version from commit history, regen changelog, commit, tag. Vars: VERSION=vX.Y.Z to override' preconditions: - sh: '[ -z "$(git status --porcelain)" ]' msg: working tree must be clean cmds: - 'NEXT={{.VERSION | default ""}}; [ -n "$NEXT" ] || NEXT=$(git cliff --bumped-version)' - git cliff --tag "$NEXT" -o CHANGELOG.md - git add CHANGELOG.md && git commit -m "chore(release): $NEXT" - git tag -a "$NEXT" -m "$NEXT" - 'echo "✓ tagged $NEXT — push with: git push --follow-tags"' docker:build: # replace existing target desc: Build the production image, version-tagged cmds: - docker build --build-arg VERSION={{.VERSION}} -t {{.IMAGE}}:{{.VERSION}} -t {{.IMAGE}}:latest . docker:push: desc: Push version + latest tags to GHCR (docker login ghcr.io first) cmds: - docker push {{.IMAGE}}:{{.VERSION}} - docker push {{.IMAGE}}:latest ``` with `IMAGE: '{{.IMAGE | default "ghcr.io/thebranchdriftcatalyst/boomtime"}}'` in `vars` (owner/name pending — see Open Questions). Note: `release` shells multiple commands — Task runs each cmd in its own shell, so compute `$NEXT` in a single cmd line or a small script; the sketch above needs the bump+cliff+commit+tag collapsed into one `cmds` entry or moved to `tools/release.sh`. git-cliff install path: `brew install git-cliff` locally (it's Rust, not `go install`-able); CI uses the official action. ## Workstream 4 — GHCR + GitHub Actions New `.github/workflows/release.yml` (on tag push): ```yaml name: release on: push: tags: ["v*"] permissions: contents: write # for the GH Release notes step packages: write # push to GHCR jobs: release: runs-on: ubuntu-latest steps: - uses: actions/checkout@v4 with: { fetch-depth: 0 } - uses: docker/login-action@v3 with: registry: ghcr.io username: ${{ github.actor }} password: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }} - uses: docker/metadata-action@v5 id: meta with: images: ghcr.io/${{ github.repository }} tags: | type=semver,pattern={{version}} type=raw,value=latest - uses: docker/build-push-action@v6 with: context: . push: true build-args: VERSION=${{ github.ref_name }} tags: ${{ steps.meta.outputs.tags }} labels: ${{ steps.meta.outputs.labels }} # optional GH Release with git-cliff notes - uses: orhun/git-cliff-action@v4 id: notes with: { args: --latest --strip header } - uses: softprops/action-gh-release@v2 with: { body: ${{ steps.notes.outputs.content }} } ``` Uses only the ephemeral `GITHUB_TOKEN` — no PAT, no baked secrets. `ghcr.io/${{ github.repository }}` auto-tracks whatever the repo ends up being named. ## Workstream 5 — Secrets safety (Dockerfile audit + .dockerignore hardening) Audit result: final runtime stage copies only `/out/boomtime` — clean. But `COPY . .` in stage 2 puts the whole context into builder layers/cache; anything not dockerignored is leakable (buildx cache export, `--target server` images). No build args exist today and none of the new ones (VERSION) are secret. All `BOOM_*` / `WAKATIME_API_KEY` / `GITHUB_TOKEN` stay runtime env (compose `env_file`, `docker run -e`). Never add secret build args; if a private-module need ever arises use `RUN --mount=type=secret`. **Exact `.dockerignore` additions** (append to current file): ``` # env & credentials — .env/.env.local already listed; broaden: .envrc .env.* !.env.example *.local.json .beads-credential-key # agent/tooling state (contains local settings, possibly tokens) .claude/ .serena/ .agents/ .codex/ .playwright-mcp/ # build-context hygiene (not secrets, just bloat / cache-busting) .github/ docs/ *.png *.md !CHANGELOG.md ov.md .air.toml docker-compose.yml Dockerfile* Taskfile.yml ``` Notes: `!.env.example` re-includes the template (harmless, though nothing in the build needs it — could also just exclude everything env-ish). **`!CHANGELOG.md` is load-bearing**: the `*.md` bloat rule must not exclude it or `go:embed` breaks the Docker build. `web/.env.example` is fine. Verify context contents after the change with a one-off audit: `docker build -q -o /dev/null - context check: printf 'FROM busybox\nCOPY . /ctx\n' | docker build -f - -t ctx-audit . && docker run --rm ctx-audit find /ctx | sort` — confirm no `.envrc`, no `settings.local.json`, no `.env`. ## Implementation order 1. `.dockerignore` hardening (independent, do first — it protects everything after). 2. `cliff.toml` + generate/commit initial `CHANGELOG.md`. 3. Go: root `embed.go`, `config.Version`, `main.go` ldflags var + cobra Version, `internal/handler/meta.go`, route registration. 4. Frontend: `lib/changelog.ts` parser + tests, `pages/Changelog.tsx`, `App.tsx` route, `AppShell.tsx` NAV + sidebar version. 5. Taskfile: `VERSION` var, `version`, `changelog`, `release`, updated `docker:build`, new `docker:push`. 6. Dockerfile: `ARG VERSION` + ldflags + OCI labels. 7. `.github/workflows/release.yml`. 8. First release: `task release` → v0.1.0 → push tag → verify GHCR image. ## Testing strategy - Go: handler tests (echo recorder) for `/api/v1/version` (JSON shape, "dev" default) and `/api/v1/changelog` (200, text/markdown, non-empty) — follow `internal/handler/*_test.go` patterns; run via `task test` (isolated boomtime_test DB; meta handlers need no DB). - Frontend: vitest for the changelog parser (versions, groups, unreleased section, running-version matching); existing `lib/*.test.ts` convention. - Build: `task build && bin/boomtime --version` prints the describe string; `task docker:build && docker run --rm --version`. - Secrets: run the ctx-audit container check above; also `docker history` the builder target to sanity-check layer sizes. - CI: push a `v0.0.1-rc` tag on a branch/fork to dry-run the workflow before the real v0.1.0. ## Risks & gotchas - `go:embed CHANGELOG.md` = compile failure anywhere the file is missing (fresh clones before first generation, and Docker if `*.md` dockerignore rule lacks the `!CHANGELOG.md` negation). Mitigate: commit CHANGELOG.md, keep the negation, CI builds catch regressions. - No tags yet → `git describe --tags` alone fails; always use `--always --dirty`. - Taskfile `dotenv: [".env"]` loads real secrets into task env — fine, but never template env values into `docker build` args. - `task release` multi-line shell var scoping (each cmd = new shell) — collapse into one cmd or a `tools/release.sh`. - Naming flux (boomtime vs gaka vs hakatime remote) affects: image name, ldflags path `main.version` (path-independent, safe), OCI source label, workflow `github.repository` (self-healing). Keep `IMAGE` a single overridable Task var so a rename is a one-line change. - `.env.*` dockerignore glob: plain `.env` entry already exists; keep both `.env` and `.env.*`. - git-cliff not installable via `go install` — brew locally, `orhun/git-cliff-action` in CI; guard the task with a `command -v` check. ## Open questions for DJ 1. **GHCR owner/name**: `ghcr.io/thebranchdriftcatalyst/` — boomtime, gakatime, or hakatime? Remote is `hakatime.git`, module is `boomtime`, local dir + beads prefix say `gaka`. Is the "gaka rename" still planned, or did `05f3639` (→ boomtime) settle it? This decides image name, OCI labels, and whether the repo gets renamed on GitHub first. 2. Should `/api/v1/version` + `/api/v1/changelog` be public (like badges) or session-authed? 3. Custom lightweight changelog parser vs adding `react-markdown` (~+30KB) — plan assumes custom parser. 4. Want the optional GH Release (release notes) step in the workflow, or GHCR push only? 5. First tag `v0.1.0` acceptable? And should `chore(beads)` commits be skipped from the changelog (plan assumes yes)? ### Critical Files for Implementation - /Users/hdaniels/catalyst-devspace/repos/gakatime/.dockerignore - /Users/hdaniels/catalyst-devspace/repos/gakatime/Dockerfile - /Users/hdaniels/catalyst-devspace/repos/gakatime/Taskfile.yml - /Users/hdaniels/catalyst-devspace/repos/gakatime/internal/server/server.go (route registration; handler in new internal/handler/meta.go) - /Users/hdaniels/catalyst-devspace/repos/gakatime/web/src/components/AppShell.tsx (NAV; plus web/src/App.tsx + new web/src/pages/Changelog.tsx)

title: Edit existing name-remapping rules in place (Settings + explorer modal)
description: The listed Name-remapping rules on Settings (and the 'Rename project' explorer modal) are currently view + delete (× ) only. Add EDIT: clicking a rule row (the > chevron) expands an inline, pre-filled RemappingForm (axis + match strategy + pattern + target) to change and Save. Reuse RemappingForm with preset props + an 'edit' mode. Stays reversible/non-destructive (raw records untouched). NOTE: shares files (Settings.tsx, RemappingForm.tsx) with gaka-r8c — do AFTER that lands to avoid edit conflicts.

title: DB observability: query logging + plan/N+1 detection wrapper
description: Wrap pgx pool operations to log every query (SQL, args, duration) specially, with dev query-plan (EXPLAIN) hooks and per-request query-count to surface N+1 patterns. Go options: pgx v5 QueryTracer interface + github.com/jackc/pgx/v5/tracelog (structured slog logging); github.com/exaring/otelpgx for OpenTelemetry spans; a request-scoped counter middleware to flag N+1 (>threshold queries per request). Evaluate + wire into internal/db pool setup + logging.
design:
RECOMMENDATION: Attach a pgx v5 QueryTracer at pool-config time. This is the single transparent choke-point — zero call-site changes. Every d.Pool.Query/QueryRow/Exec (and tx.Exec) already flows through the pgx tracer hooks, and all call sites already pass a request-scoped ctx (handlers use c.Request().Context()), so log + N+1 + EXPLAIN all attach here for free.

INTEGRATION POINT (our code): internal/db/db.go, func New(ctx, url). Today it calls pgxpool.New(ctx, url) with NO config. Change to:
  cfg, err := pgxpool.ParseConfig(url)
  cfg.ConnConfig.Tracer = <our tracer>
  pool, err := pgxpool.NewWithConfig(ctx, cfg)
The DB struct exposes Pool *pgxpool.Pool as a PUBLIC field used verbatim across sessions.go/curation.go/etc. — we do NOT wrap or replace it, we only enrich its ConnConfig. Add an optional cfg/opts param to db.New (or a db.NewWithObservability) so cmd/gakatime/main.go can pass env-driven settings; tests (testutil.go, main_test.go) keep the plain path.

TRACER COMPOSITION: use github.com/jackc/pgx/v5/multitracer.New(...) to combine three QueryTracers on the one ConnConfig.Tracer slot:
  1. tracelog.TraceLog{Logger: slogAdapter, LogLevel: ...}  -> structured query logging (SQL, args, duration, commandTag, pid) via our existing log/slog default logger.
  2. n1Tracer (custom, ~40 lines) -> request-scoped query counter for N+1 detection.
  3. planTracer (custom, dev-only) -> auto-EXPLAIN on slow SELECTs.
multitracer.New auto-splits by interface, so tracelog also picks up Batch/Connect/CopyFrom/Prepare/Acquire/Release for free.

slog ADAPTER (grounds in our logging.go default slog): tracelog.Logger is one method: Log(ctx, level tracelog.LogLevel, msg string, data map[string]any). Adapt with tracelog.LoggerFunc:
  var slogAdapter = tracelog.LoggerFunc(func(ctx, lvl, msg, data){
     attrs := redact(data)            // drop/mask sensitive args
     slog.Default().Log(ctx, mapLevel(lvl), msg, attrs...)
  })
mapLevel: tracelog LogLevelError->slog.Error, Warn->Warn, Info->Info, Debug/Trace->Debug.

N+1 COUNTER (threads through ctx we already have): middleware (echo) stashes a *reqStats{count int; byNorm map[string]int} via context.WithValue at request start. TraceQueryStart increments count and a normalized-SQL bucket (strip literals/whitespace, collapse IN-lists); at request end the middleware logs WARN if count>threshold or any single normalized statement ran >nearIdenticalThreshold times (classic N+1: same statement in a loop). pgx passes the ctx from TraceQueryStart through to TraceQueryEnd, and our handlers already pass the request ctx into d.Pool.Query, so no signature changes.

DEV PLAN CAPTURE (planTracer): in TraceQueryStart stash the SQL+args+start-time in the returned ctx; in TraceQueryEnd, if dev-flag set AND duration>threshold AND statement is a read (SQL starts with SELECT/WITH, case-insensitive, after trimming) -> run `EXPLAIN (FORMAT TEXT) <sql>` (NOT EXPLAIN ANALYZE, and NEVER on writes) on a FRESH pool conn with the SAME args, log plan at WARN. Must guard against recursion (the EXPLAIN query itself fires the tracer — tag its ctx with a skip flag) and never run inside the caller`s tx.

GOTCHAS (call out in impl):
 - REDACTION IS MANDATORY. Token/password/salt values flow as $-args into d.Pool: sessions.go GetUserByToken/GetUserByRefreshToken/InsertAPIToken/CreateAccessTokens/DeleteTokens (token, refresh_token) and users (hashed_password, salt_used). Raw arg logging leaks them. Default to LOGGING ARGS ONLY IN DEV, and even then redact by matching the SQL (tables auth_tokens/refresh_tokens/users or columns token/password/salt) -> replace those positional args with "***". Safest default: log arg COUNT + types in prod, full args only in dev after redaction.
 - Tracer fires for EVERY query incl. migrations, Ping, pool warmup, and the internal EXPLAIN — filter/skip these (skip flag in ctx; migrations run via a separate path in migrate.go so mostly N/A for the pool).
 - EXPLAIN ANALYZE actually executes -> forbidden for writes; we use plain EXPLAIN and gate to reads only.
 - N+1 counter needs the middleware installed on the echo server (internal/server) so ctx carries reqStats; queries outside an HTTP request (import worker, CLI create-user) simply have no counter in ctx -> no-op, which is fine.
notes:
LIBRARY OPTIONS + CITATIONS (pgx v5 v5.10.0 already in go.mod):

1. pgx v5 QueryTracer (native hook — the choke-point). Interface:
   TraceQueryStart(ctx, *pgx.Conn, TraceQueryStartData{SQL string; Args []any}) context.Context
   TraceQueryEnd(ctx, *pgx.Conn, TraceQueryEndData{CommandTag pgconn.CommandTag; Err error})
   ctx returned by Start is passed to End -> stash start-time/req-state there.
   src: https://github.com/jackc/pgx/blob/master/tracer.go ; docs: https://pkg.go.dev/github.com/jackc/pgx/v5

2. github.com/jackc/pgx/v5/tracelog (SHIPS WITH pgx — no new dep) -> structured logging.
   TraceLog{Logger; LogLevel; Config} implements QueryTracer + Batch/Connect/CopyFrom/Prepare/Acquire/Release tracers.
   Logger iface: Log(ctx, level LogLevel, msg string, data map[string]any). Adapter type LoggerFunc.
   LogLevels: Trace=6,Debug=5,Info=4,Warn=3,Error=2,None=1. Logs SQL, args, time/duration, commandTag, pid.
   -> wrap our log/slog default via tracelog.LoggerFunc. docs: https://pkg.go.dev/github.com/jackc/pgx/v5/tracelog

3. github.com/jackc/pgx/v5/multitracer (SHIPS WITH pgx) -> combine tracers on one ConnConfig.Tracer.
   New(tracers ...pgx.QueryTracer) *Tracer, auto-splits by interface. Lets tracelog + custom N+1 + custom EXPLAIN (+ otelpgx later) coexist. docs: https://pkg.go.dev/github.com/jackc/pgx/v5/multitracer

4. github.com/exaring/otelpgx (OPTIONAL, future) -> OTel spans per query; same hook: cfg.ConnConfig.Tracer = otelpgx.NewTracer(); pgxpool.NewWithConfig(...). Also RecordStats(pool) for pool metrics. Requires recent Go. repo: https://github.com/exaring/otelpgx ; docs: https://pkg.go.dev/github.com/exaring/otelpgx ; alt: https://github.com/pgx-contrib/pgxotel
   Decision: NOT needed for phase 1 (no OTel collector wired yet). Defer; the multitracer slot makes adding it trivial later.

5. N+1 detection: no dedicated Go lib — standard pattern is a custom QueryTracer + request-scoped counter in context (increment in TraceQueryStart, evaluate at request end, WARN on count>N or same-normalized-SQL>M). Confirmed viable because our handlers already thread c.Request().Context() into every d.Pool call. ref/background: https://github.com/jackc/pgx/discussions/1677 , https://github.com/jackc/pgx/issues/2021

6. EXPLAIN capture: also custom (no built-in). Re-run plain EXPLAIN (never ANALYZE, never on writes) on a fresh conn in TraceQueryEnd when dev-flag set and duration>threshold.

ENV FLAGS (follow config.go HAKA_* + getEnvBool/getEnvInt convention; gate off cfg.Env=="dev"/IsDev()):
  HAKA_DB_LOG_QUERIES   (bool, default false in prod / on in dev) — enable tracelog query logging.
  HAKA_DB_LOG_ARGS      (bool, default false) — log (redacted) args; dev-only recommended.
  HAKA_DB_N1_THRESHOLD  (int, default 20) — queries/request to WARN.
  HAKA_DB_N1_DUP_THRESHOLD (int, default 10) — identical normalized statements/request.
  HAKA_DB_EXPLAIN_SLOW_MS (int, default 0=off; dev-only) — auto-EXPLAIN reads slower than this.

PHASED BREAKDOWN (ship incrementally; each phase independently valuable):
  Phase 1 — Query logging: switch db.New to ParseConfig+NewWithConfig; add slog->tracelog.LoggerFunc adapter; attach tracelog.TraceLog via multitracer; implement redaction (mask token/password/salt args by SQL match); wire HAKA_DB_LOG_QUERIES / HAKA_DB_LOG_ARGS. Verify migrations/Ping noise is acceptable/filtered.
  Phase 2 — N+1 counter: echo middleware stashes reqStats in ctx; custom n1Tracer increments count + normalized-SQL buckets in TraceQueryStart; middleware logs WARN at request end on threshold; HAKA_DB_N1_THRESHOLD / HAKA_DB_N1_DUP_THRESHOLD.
  Phase 3 — Dev plan capture: custom planTracer; on slow read in TraceQueryEnd, run EXPLAIN on fresh conn with recursion-skip flag; reads-only guard; HAKA_DB_EXPLAIN_SLOW_MS.
  Phase 4 (optional/future) — otelpgx into the same multitracer once an OTel pipeline exists.

RISKS: sensitive-arg leakage (mitigated by mandatory redaction + args-off-by-default in prod); tracer overhead on hot heartbeat-ingest path (keep logging at Debug, N+1 counter O(1) map ops); EXPLAIN re-run cost/recursion (dev-only + skip flag + reads-only); noise from non-request queries (import worker/CLI have no reqStats ctx -> N+1 no-ops safely).
